title: Item default value - same on all boards from same template

posts.author: TWolf (Torsten Wolf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there,
i’m using templates to create my new boards and do also use linked columns to my highlevel board. I really like the Idea of the Item Default Value, but it looks like it has a Bug

If i change the Item Default Value in a Board created from a Template, this value is also changed for all other Boards created from the same Template. This would make sense if i change the value directly in the Template, but not in a Board craeted from the Template, right ?

So is this a Bug or a Feature ?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Torsten,

I’ve tested this on my end and this indeed sounds a little strange.

When I update the item default value in Board 1 (created from template), it does not impact the values in Board 2 (created from same template).

Would you be happy to send me a direct message including a quick video demonstration of this behaviour in action?

Can you please also test if this behaviour is replicated in an incognito window?

Thanks for your help!

posts.author: dfhimz (David F.)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello! I have the exact same issue. Has there been any solution to this issue?

posts.author: TWolf (Torsten Wolf)
posts.cookedHtml: Right now this is under Investigation from the Support Team.

posts.author: derek.myers12 (Derek Myers)
posts.cookedHtml: I also have the same issue. Following along for the resolution.

posts.author: ksnavely (Kyle Snavely)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here. It’s inconsistent as well.

Trying to create a high-level/low-level board system. I followed the tutorial exactly:

  1. Created a template and automation in the high-level portfolio board so when an item’s status is changed to “Launch,” it creates a new board using the template.
  2. In the new board (low-level) created from the template, I created my action items, selected all, and linked them to my project portfolio (high-level) board item.
  3. Then, assigned the default item value to link any new items created in the low-level board to the project portfolio item in the high-level board.

The first time I tested this it worked. All subsequent tests however, when I assign the default item value to link to the portfolio, it changes both the template default, as well as any/all other boards created from the template.

Is there an update on this? It’s a critical blocker for my team as we’re trying to switch to this high-level/low-level board system and if the high-level board does not update automatically when new items are added to low-level boards (or worse, all low-level board data is changed to display only one low-level board’s items), then this isn’t a feasible solution.

Please let me know if you need additional information re: the steps I took, what order, etc.

Thanks

posts.author: TWolf (Torsten Wolf)
posts.cookedHtml:
I do not have any update on this.
@BiancaT Do you have any update?

posts.author: ksnavely (Kyle Snavely)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT

We’re at a critical moment in our team’s project management maturity. I’m really needing this function to operate correctly!

posts.cookedHtml: Hi, adding my team to the list of those who are having this issue. Has anyone figured out a solution (or workaround)?
